title: commiphora horrida
intro: Commiphora horrida, also known as the 'grub tree' or 'stick tree', is a species of flowering plant in the Burseraceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Commiphora horrida include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a herbal infusion, ointment, or powder for various uses, including topical applications and traditional remedies.
